Dashboard Archive: 2026-03-20 Home
About This Dashboard
What It Does
Short-term momentum ranker scoring 700+ tickers on returns (1d to 1y), SMA gate (above 30/50/100/200 day), ratio consistency across timeframes, and resilience on bad SPY days. Higher composite scores indicate stronger, more consistent short-term momentum.
Today's Reading
# Market Analysis: momentum-ranker Today's data reveals two exceptional short-term momentum leaders with strikingly different risk profiles. TYRA ranks #1 with a composite score of 97.8, showing smooth, consistent gains across all timeframes (1.61% daily to 247.63% annual) with perfectly balanced momentum ratios (all five ratio passes) and strong resilience during market downturns (bad_spy_score of 0.686). In stark contrast, TNGX displays extreme volatility—while posting a spectacular 1020.23% one-year return, it shows chaotic ratio patterns (a wild 38.71x jump from 1-day to 1-week returns, then collapsing to 1.21x from 6-month to 3-month) and significantly lower downside resilience (bad_spy_score of only 0.341), suggesting it may struggle during SPY sell-offs. Both securities cleared all SMA gates and maintain above-average correlations to tech (QQQ at 0.197 for TYRA), though TNGX's extreme volatility and the divergence between its extraordinary annual returns and deteriorating intermediate momentum ratios warrant caution about sustainability.
For educational and informational purposes only. Not financial advice. Past performance does not guarantee future results.
